posts._note: Talk about the problem. No link in first 3-5 lines. Make them feel the frustration. Then reveal the guide.
posts.linkedin:
You have five possible niches in your head.

You've been thinking about them for months.

And the longer you think — the more paralyzed you feel. Because what if you pick the wrong one? What if you spend 6 months building in the wrong direction? What if you niche down too far and the audience is too small?

So you keep researching. Keep watching "how to find your niche" videos. Keep telling yourself you'll decide next week.

Here's the truth: niche confusion is not a creativity problem. It is a filtering problem.

You don't have too few ideas. You have too many — and no objective system for evaluating them against each other.

The S.D.I.™ Formula (Skills × Demand × Interest) applies three scoring criteria simultaneously to all of your candidate niches and produces a ranked, data-backed decision in 7 days.

Free guide. No email required.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/i-dont-know-my-niche

posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
Here's the framework that ends niche confusion in 7 days.

The S.D.I.™ Formula — Skills × Demand × Interest:

S — Skills
What you can teach at depth. Include professional experience, self-taught expertise, and anything you've helped others with — paid or unpaid. The key question: could you explain something about this topic that most content in this space gets wrong? If yes, you have a unique content angle.

D — Demand
What the market actively searches for and pays for. Not just "people are interested" — but actively spending money to solve this problem. Use four free tools:
• Google Trends: upward trajectory over 12 months = positive signal
• YouTube Search: high views on channels with low subscribers = gap confirmed
• Reddit/Quora: active frustrated questions = demand signal
• Gumroad/Etsy: bestselling digital products in the niche = monetization proven

I — Interest
What you can sustain for 3–5 years without external motivation. Three tests:
→ The 2AM Test: Would you still research this topic at 2AM with no audience and no income?
→ The Annoyance Reaction: Do you get frustrated when you see bad advice in this space?
→ The Years Test: Can you generate 50+ content ideas in 20 minutes?

The niche at the intersection of all three — scoring 7+ on each dimension — is your profitable niche.

A niche that scores 9/9/3 will burn you out before it rewards you.
A niche that scores 9/3/9 will produce content no one discovers.
A niche that scores 3/9/9 will be unsustainable to deliver.

All three must be present. That's the formula.

👉 Free guide: https://publixion.com/guides/i-dont-know-my-niche

#NicheSelection #PersonalBrand #ContentStrategy #CreatorEconomy #Publixion

🔷 INTEREST — What you can sustain for years. The 2AM Test: would you still research this with zero income and zero audience?

Score each niche candidate 1–10 across all three.

posts._note: Call out specific types of people this guide is for. Be precise — specificity sells.
posts.linkedin:
The I Don't Know My Niche free guide is for a specific type of person.

You'll recognize yourself if:

→ You've been trying to find your niche for 3+ months and are still in research mode

→ You have multiple areas of knowledge and can't commit to one because every option feels equally viable — or equally risky

→ You're posting content sporadically across different topics and getting little traction because your audience can't figure out what you stand for

→ You know you want to build a personal brand, grow an audience, or freelance — but you're stuck at the very first step

→ You've started building in one direction and then pivoted to another because the first one "didn't feel right" — without knowing what "right" actually means

→ You're a professional with real expertise who wants to turn it into content, clients, or a digital product — but doesn't know which aspect of your knowledge to focus on

This guide is not for people who already have a clear niche and want to grow it. That's a content strategy conversation.

This is for the stuck person at Step 0 — who needs a decision-making system, not more inspiration about "following your passion."

Free. 8 pages. 7-day execution calendar included.

posts._note: Show how a specific person would use this guide step by step. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
Here's what 7 days inside the S.D.I.™ framework looks like in practice.

Case study: Priya. UX designer. 6 years experience. Wanted to build a personal brand and eventually freelance. Had three niche candidates she'd been debating for 4 months:

→ UI design tutorials (high skill, medium demand, low interest — she'd been doing it all day for 6 years)
→ Career transition from graphic design to UX (medium skill, high demand, high interest)
→ UX for e-commerce (high skill, high demand, medium interest)

She applied the S.D.I.™ scoring matrix:

UI Design Tutorials: S=9, D=6, I=3 → Total: 18. Below threshold.
UX for E-commerce: S=8, D=8, I=6 → Total: 22. Strong candidate.
Career Transition to UX: S=7, D=8, I=9 → Total: 24. Clear winner.

DAY 5 — She published 2 test posts on the career transition niche.
Within 48 hours: 6 replies. 3 questions about how she made the switch. 2 DMs asking if she offered coaching.

DAY 7 — She completed her positioning statement:
"I create content for graphic designers transitioning into UX roles who are overwhelmed by where to start — sharing the exact portfolio, skills, and mindset shifts that helped me land my first UX job in 90 days."

3 content pillars: Portfolio building, Tool tutorials, Career transition stories.

Month 1 result: 5,000 LinkedIn impressions per post. 3 coaching inquiry DMs. A waitlist for a mini-course she hadn't built yet.

The niche had been there all along. She just needed a system to see it.

👉 Free guide: https://publixion.com/guides/i-dont-know-my-niche

#NicheSelection #PersonalBrand #ContentStrategy #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
The four objections I hear before people download the niche guide — answered.

❌ "I'm afraid I'll pick the wrong niche."
✅ The S.D.I.™ framework doesn't ask you to pick forever — it asks you to commit to a 90-day test. The worst niche you test produces more learning than the best niche you research indefinitely. Data from a published post tells you more than any amount of internal deliberation. And niches are not prisons — they are starting positions.

❌ "I have too many interests. I can't choose just one."
✅ That's exactly what the scoring matrix is for. When you apply the same 3 criteria to all of your interests simultaneously, the scores make the decision — not your feelings. Multiple high-interest options competing for attention is a filtering problem. The framework provides the filter.

❌ "I don't know if there's an audience for my niche."
✅ The demand evaluation section of the guide uses four free tools — Google Trends, YouTube search, Reddit, and Gumroad — to verify whether an audience exists before you create a single piece of content. You test demand before you commit. No guessing.

❌ "I've tried picking a niche before and pivoted after a month."
✅ You pivoted because you picked based on feeling rather than data. The S.D.I.™ process ensures your niche candidate passes three objective filters before you commit. A niche that scores 22/30 doesn't feel uncertain — it feels obvious. That's the difference.

This guide is free.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/i-dont-know-my-niche

#NicheSelection #PersonalBrand #ContentStrategy #Publixion
